subject: Upgrade SDC from Titan to Janus Graph

see details in https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/xtesting-onap/-/jobs/234026193/artifacts/download
I captured the logs
I got "Deployment onap-sdc-******  is not ready"
I can send you the logs

It would be interesting to rebase the patch.
I will give a try to other patchs and see if this error is specific to this patch

Patch Set 1:

the gating process was more successful (no SDC pods failing) with recent patches: 89710, 90029, 90033,  	90050 
it seems to indicate that this change from Titan to Janus has side effects on SDC.
It may be due to the patch and/or to the configuration of the installation (override)

could you rebase the patch and re-try

Patch Set 3:

I have some doubt on the installation performed in the last gating.
We encountered strange behavior since few days on our installations. 
It can be ignored.

However the first attempts were more trustable (it means that other patch just before and after were fully successful) but this patch lead to SDC pods and healthcheck errors.

i hope we could progress on installation issue to give a new try

Patch Set 3:

Morgan, please clarify what are your concerns? Was the last deploy done with the latest SDC images (1.5 STAGE)? How do you deploy the SDC, sdc Cassandra or Cassandra common?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Morgan, please clarify what are your concerns? Was the last deploy done with the latest SDC images (1.5 STAGE)? How do you deploy the SDC, sdc Cassandra or Cassandra common?

SDC images (1.5 STAGE)? How do you deploy the SDC, sdc Cassandra or Cassandra common?

yes the images used nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/sdc-frontend:1.5-STAGING-latest
you can have all the details in https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/xtesting-onap/-/jobs/249303982/artifacts/download results/k8s/onap-k8s.log

my concern on the last run is not ONAP itself but some troubles we have with kubernetes since few days and more precisely on the IPS. One of the consequences is: some pods and specific nodes cannot reach other pods and then cannot start properly. Our deployement deals with 12 compute nodes, randomly we regulary have 2 or 3 of the compute nodes facing this issue.
we are facing weird behaviors since few days on all our CI chains.
That is why I assume the last gating results could be misleading and problems could be due to kubernetes and not to ONAP.

However the first gating done mid of June (19th of June) were more stable and trustable And they showed errors in SDC due to the patch. But this iteration was done with version 1.4.1
you can also have access to the test results and logs of this iteration here: https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/xtesting-onap/-/jobs/234592199/artifacts/download

to summarize I can unfortunately not vote for the moment. The gating does not help me
first iterations were trustable and false but should be fixed with the upgrade of the version.
But gating on the good version seems no more trustable. We are investigating on it.

Patch Set 3:

Morgan, any updates on this commit?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

This commits it hanging for over a month. Please merge it and unblock the SDC team

Patch Set 3: Verified+1 Code-Review+2

Posting test results of successful ONAP deployment and SDC health check would go long way to speed up this patch merge.
Currently, since the gating is not trustworthy according to Morgan, I am merging the change (there is no logic there that would cause me not to approve) and SDC team is responsible to fix whatever may fail.
